2001-08-01.title: At Least 60 Die as Landslides Ravage an Indonesian Island
2001-08-01.abstract: Huge floods and landslides poured down mountainsides early this morning on a remote Indonesian island, sweeping away entire villages and causing at least 60 deaths, local officials said

2001-08-01.title: Austerity Plan In Argentina Provokes Wide Protests
2001-08-01.abstract: Tens of thousands of unemployed workers, public employees and students block major highways and city streets across Argentina to protest new government spending cuts aimed at averting foreign debt default; coordinated protests, led by small but well organized leftist groups, are another sign that unrest is growing in response to three-year recession; photo (M)

2001-08-01.title: G.O.P. Delays Effort to Give Bush Latitude On Free Trade
2001-08-01.abstract: House Republican leaders delay until fall effort to give president broad authority to negotiate new trade accords that could not be amended by Congress; say they lack votes to pass measure; decision is setback for Pres Bush (M)

2001-08-01.title: World Briefing | Europe: Northern Ireland: Proposals For I.R.A.
2001-08-01.abstract: British and Irish governments make public non-negotiable final package of proposals aimed at persuading Irish Republican Army to begin disarming to prevent collapse of power-sharing Northern Ireland Assembly set up by 1998 peace agreement (S)

2001-08-01.title: World Briefing | Europe: Austria: Payment In Nazi Case
2001-08-01.abstract: Austria makes first payment of $36 million to former Nazi slaves and forced laborers who worked in country during World War II (S)

2001-08-02.title: World Briefing | Asia: India: Prime Minister To Stay
2001-08-02.abstract: India's prime minister, Atal Behari Vajpayee, withdraws threat to resign after members of his ruling coalition rally behind him and issue statement of 'unqualified' faith and trust in his leadership; photo (S)

2001-08-02.title: Verdict Is Due Today in Major Trial on War Crimes in Bosnia
2001-08-02.abstract: Mark Harmon, American prosecutor, ends his final plea in major trial on war crimes in Bosnia; case is against general at Srebrenica, Radislav Krstic, and reported massacre of 7,000 Muslims by Bosnian Serbs who overran city in July 1995; verdict will represent Hague tribunal's first ruling on whether genocide was committed in Bosnia in war from 1992 to 1995; tribunal investigators have exhumed 2,028 bodies from mass graves in region; additional 2,500 have been located; Krstic is also charged with mass deportation of civilians; he has pleaded not guilty and faulted others, pointing to Gen Ratko Mladic, overall Bosnian Serb military commander (M)

2001-08-02.title: Senate Committee United in Seeking Bush Action on Emissions
2001-08-02.abstract: Senate committee unanimously approves nonbinding resolution calling on Bush administration to develop plan to reduce emissions of greenhouse gases and to take part in international negotiations on global warming; Sen John Kerry, Democrat, says purpose of resolution is to make clear that Bush administration cannot be 'sitting on the sidelines'; resolution is carefully written to allow administration supporters to feel comfortable voting for it (M)

2001-08-02.title: World Briefing | Europe: Italy: Debate Over G-8 Meeting
2001-08-02.abstract: Italy's interior minister, Claudio Scajola, survives vote of confidence in conservative-dominated Senate, while accusations continue over violence at G-8 summit in Genoa; lower house of Parliament approves independent investigation into events, in which police shot and killed protestor (S)

2001-08-02.title: Senate Approves Limits on Mexican Truckers
2001-08-02.abstract: Senate approves $60 billion transportation bill that Pres Bush has threatened to veto because it would block his plan to allow Mexican trucks to have widespread access to American roads beginning next year; White House says restrictions in Senate bill involving Mexican trucks violates North American Free Trade Agreement and endangers his goal of improved diplomatic and trade ties with Mexico; Sen Trent Lott vows that Republicans will find votes to sustain White House veto of bill (M)

2001-08-03.title: Mistakes Cited in Downing of Plane in Peru
2001-08-03.abstract: Joint US-Peruvian inquiry finds that disastrous series of mistakes led to shooting down in April of small plane carrying missionary family in Peru by Peruvian fighter involved in joint US-Peruvian antidrug operation; cites language problems, poor communications and shortcuts in following established procedures; American missionary Veronica Bowers and her infant daughter Charity, were killed; pilot Kevin Donaldson was injured (M)

2001-08-03.title: Ousted President Returns to Indonesia in Defiant Mood
2001-08-03.abstract: Former President Abdurrahman Wahid returned home today after a medical checkup in the United States, still sounding angry over his ouster

2001-08-03.title: Gephardt Launches an Attack on Bush's Foreign Policy
2001-08-03.abstract: Rep Richard A Gephardt accuses Bush administration of obsession with missile defense and of pursuing unilateralist approach to world affairs that risks antagonizing Russia and undermining relations with Europe; vows to unite Democrats and persuade Republicans to forge bipartisan majority in Congress to block any missile defense system that would violate 1972 Antiballistic Missile Treaty; defends formal arms control negotiations as essential to maintaining nuclear peace; says NATO allies are worried that Bush administration has embraced go-it-alone policy that undermines international security, hurts economic and environmental interests and prevents US from seizing opportunity for engagement with Russia (M)

2001-08-03.title: Turkey's Ruling Coalition Uneasily Awaits Arrival of New Party
2001-08-03.abstract: Turkey's coalition government faces new challenge from populist-oriented party being formed from remains of outlawed Islamic-oriented party; new party boasts popular former mayor of Istanbul as its leader, Recep Tayyip Erdogan, and promises to broaden its appeal beyond religious voters; Erdogan spent four months in jail and was barred from politics for five years; ban on his political participation was lifted in last year's general amnesty (M)

2001-08-03.title: U.S. Scholar Held in Russia May Be Freed, Officials Hint
2001-08-03.abstract: Russian judicial authorities indicate that John E Tobin, American Fulbright scholar imprisoned on marijuana-related charges in Voronezh since Feb, will probably be freed from prison in next few days; decision to grant Tobin parole would resolve one of oddest disputes between Russia and US in years; Tobin's arrest turned into minor sensation when local officials of Russia's counterintelligence agency claimed he was in training to become American spy and that they had thwarted espionage plot; photo (M)

2001-08-04.title: Rumsfeld Says Iraq Has Improved Air Defenses Since February
2001-08-04.abstract: Defense Sec Donald H Rumsfeld says Iraq has rebuilt and even improved its air defenses since American and British planes flew strikes against radar stations and command centers in Feb, news conf; administration officials say Iraq has become more aggressive and effective in targeting American and British planes over no-flight zones, and has even fired at American planes in airspace of neighboring countries; Rumsfeld refuses to comment on any specific plans to strike again at Iraqi targets (M)

2001-08-04.title: Article 2001080490913439223 -- No Title
2001-08-04.abstract: Find a Job            Post a Job            Find a Home            Personals            All Classifieds                International          -     Africa          -     Americas          -     Asia Pacific          -     Europe          -    Middle East           National            Politics            Business            Technology            Science            Health            Arts            Sports            New York Region            Weather            Obituaries            NYT Front Page            Corrections                 Editorials/Op-Ed            Readers' Opinions

2001-08-04.title: World Briefing | Europe: Germany: Immigration Proposal
2001-08-04.abstract: Interior Min Otto Schily presents outlines of new immigration law to Germany's parliament, intending to resolve national debate on how to balance Germany's need to modernize with some Germans' fear of increasingly multicultural society (S)

2001-08-04.title: Shashemene Journal; Uneasy Bond Inside a Promised Land
2001-08-04.abstract: Rastafarianism's true believers remain in Shashemene, Ethiopia, clinging to their beliefs that late Emperor Haile Selassie I is God and Ethiopia is their promised land; they are mostly from Caribbean but also from United States and Britain; their numbers have shrunk to about 500; their many problems with Ethiopians attest to uneasy relationship that often develops between Africans and non-African blacks seeking spiritual home there; mutual alienation is deep, because Rastafarians hold beliefs and engage in practices that are directly against those of most Ethiopians; photo; map (M)

2001-08-04.title: Thailand's Popular Premier Cleared in Corruption Case
2001-08-04.abstract: Thailand's highest court acquits Prime Min Thaksin Shinawatra of financial irregularities, preserving his political career; ruling seen as test of country's evolution toward rule of law; Thaksin had overwhelming support of public; 15-member Constitutional Court was under intense pressure to acquit him, despite what Thaksin himself has admitted was clear violation of financial-disclosure laws; Thaksin, now freed from threat of removal, will be under intense pressure to bring country out of its economic slump; photo (M)

2001-08-05.title: British Immigration Aides Accused of Bias by Gypsys
2001-08-05.abstract: Richard Samko, Gypsy and reporter at Czech television, has stirred political storm, exposing in broadcast what he calls bias by British immigration officials at Prague airport against darker-skinned Gypsies; Czechs agreed to let British set up office at airport after Britain threatened to reimpose visa requirement because 2,000 Czech Gypsies applied for asylum last year; Britain has granted asylum to few Czech Gypsies, despite widespread discrimination against them in Czech Republic; photo (M)

2001-08-05.title: Brazil Steps on U.S. Toes With a Plan for Fighter Jets
2001-08-05.abstract: Brazil has set aside $700 million to buy up to 24 supersonic fighter jets; is demanding any supplier provide advanced avionics and let domestic aerospace industry build planes in Brazil; bold requirement puts Brazil in direct conflict with United States, which does not sell its most sophisticated weaponry in Latin America or transfer technology; France may be willing to meet demands, enabling Brazil to make cheaper fighter plane for sale in third world and undermine US use of arms sales to sway allies and tilt regional balances of power (M)

2001-08-05.title: Israeli Missiles Miss Leader Of Uprising; Aide Injured
2001-08-05.abstract: Israeli Army fires missiles at convoy carrying Marwan Barghouti, West Bank leader of Palestinian uprising, in what he calls attempt to assassinate him; Israelis later deny he was target, implying they were striking at his aide, Mohanad Abu Halawa, who was hurt in attack and is wanted for role in anti-Israel violence; Israelis fear new terrorist attacks in wake of recent arrests of two Palestinians carrying bombs (M)

2001-08-05.title: Derek Freeman, Who Challenged Margaret Mead on Samoa, Dies at 84
2001-08-05.abstract: Derek Freeman, professor of anthropology at Australian National University, dies at age 84; photo (M)

2001-08-05.title: Drug Use Begetting AIDS in Central Asia
2001-08-05.abstract: drug use begetting aids in central asi

2001-08-06.title: Brazilians Uneasy Despite Help by I.M.F.
2001-08-06.abstract: $15 billion in loans unexpectedly offered Brazil by International Monetary Fund on August 3 puts some distance between country and troubled neighbor Argentina; financial analysts predict deal is likely to fall short of establishing foolproof fire wall from economic troubles in Argentina that Brazil has been seeking; fund's latest support for Brazil is result of deepening recession in Argentina and of growing fears that its government will default on its $128 billion in debt (M)

2001-08-06.title: Ex-Ruling Party in Mexico Tries to Halt Skid
2001-08-06.abstract: Institutional Revolutionary Party, Mexico's former governing party, seeks to end 13-month losing streak in vote for governor of Tabasco state; party's candidate Manuel Andrade won close and hotly dispute contest in October 2000, but federal court overturned victory saying election was marred by fraud and errors (S)

2001-08-06.title: U.S. Attempt to Quiet Fighting in Macedonia May Backfire
2001-08-06.abstract: Bush administration's decision in June to freeze assets of people in Kosovo, Serbia, it considered to be supporting ethnic Albanian rebels in Macedonia, seems to be backfiring as US attempts to broker cease-fire between rebels and Macedonian government forces (M)

2001-08-06.title: Ulster Protestants' Leader Rejects I.R.A. Plan on Arms
2001-08-06.abstract: David Trimble, leader of the Ulster Unionists, said he wants to see the I.R.A. beginning to disarm rather than just announcing their willingness to do so

2001-08-06.title: Once-Thriving Sri Lankan Town a War Ruin
2001-08-06.abstract: Recent events in Sri Lanka, where almost two decades of warfare have sent hundreds of thousands of people fleeing for their lives have stirred menacing sense of foreboding; effort to restart peace talks has failed, country has plunged into political turmoil and surprise rebel attack on country's international airport and adjacent military air base near capital of Colombo have almost completed collapse into despair; fighting in and around Chavakachcheri, once-tidy and bustling town located in heart of ethnically Tamil homeland that rebels have fought for over past 18 years, described; town stands as crumbled monument to fratricidal war that has killed more than 62,000 people and is destroying very land government and rebels claim they are fighting to save; map; photo (M)

2001-08-07.title: Shenzhen Journal; Golf Obsesses a Boom Town's Elite
2001-08-07.abstract: Golf catches on in China's southern boom city of Shenzhen, where it has become obsession among elite; city is ringed by golf courses of varying size and quality, most built to cater mainly to Hong Kong residents; clubs like Mission Hills Golf Club, China's only international-standard golf resort, are springing up and signing up more local people; maps; photo (M)

2001-08-07.title: U.S. Reports Progress in Fighting Bias; Rights Groups Are Critical
2001-08-07.abstract: Bush administration, in first report by US to UN body that monitors discrimination worldwide, says US has made significant strides in reducing discrimination against minorities; Ralph Boyd Jr, director of civil rights division of Justice Department who presented report in Geneva, notes African-Americans serve in highest levels of government in significant numbers, as well as in numerous other professions (M)

2001-08-07.title: World Briefing | Asia: Indonesia: Suharto Son Accused
2001-08-07.abstract: Indonesia police declare that Hutomo Mandala Putra, fugitive son of former Pres Suharto, is a suspect in recent assassination of judge who sentenced him last year to 18 months in prison for corruption (S)

2001-08-07.title: World Briefing | Asia: Indonesia: Ex-Masseur Sentenced
World Briefing | Europe: Russia: Scholar Not Bitter
2001-08-07.abstract: Father of John E Tobin Jr, paroled from Russian prison, says his son still loves Russia and is not bitter about his six months in prison, even though he maintains he was wrongly convicted, news conf, Moscow; photo (S)

2001-08-08.title: Italy Holding a New Jersey Woman, 21, Snared in the Genoa Fracas
2001-08-08.abstract: Susanna Thomas, American jailed in Italy since her July 22 arrest outside Genoa, calls home to New Jersey for first time; is charged with conspiracy in street disorders linked to summit meeting of major industrial nations; her lawyer says police are trying to link Thomas, who is Quaker, to Black Bloc anarchist group, which clashed with police in Genoa; Sens Robert G Torricelli and Jon S Corzine ask American charge' d'affaires William P Pope to intercede; photo (M)

2001-08-08.title: A More Assertive Khatami Is Sworn In as President of Iran
2001-08-08.abstract: In his address, President Mohammad Khatami warned opponents against blocking reforms clearly demanded by the Iranian people

2001-08-08.title: After Protest, British Halt Screening of Travelers in Prague
2001-08-08.abstract: Britain to withdraw its immigration officers screening travelers at Prague airport after politicians and human rights groups charge they deliberately barred Czech Gypsies from traveling to Britain; holds three-week-old controls are being ended because they have successfully halted flood of Czech asylum seekers, almost all of whom were Gypsies; denies racial bias and reserves right to reimpose controls (M)

2001-08-08.title: World Briefing | Asia: India: Pakistani Criticized
2001-08-08.abstract: India's Prime Min Atal Behari Vajpayee, in speech before Parliament, blames Pakistan's military ruler Gen Pervez Musharraf for failure of recent summit in Agra; asserts that Musharraf has poor grasp of history and little understanding of diplomatic protocol (S)

2001-08-08.title: Power-Sharing Plans in Doubt as Ulster Peace Talks Falter
2001-08-08.abstract: With peace negotiations stalemated, Britain faced the growing likelihood today of having to put the power-sharing Northern Ireland government on hold by this weekend

2001-08-09.title: Tel Aviv Journal; The Doves of Yesteryear Haven't All Taken Flight
2001-08-09.abstract: Israel's peace movement, after months of being virtually silenced by escalating violence and heavy popularity of Prime Min Ariel Sharon's policies, has begun to stir, not dramatically but enough to be noticed; several thousand people turn out in Tel Aviv for Peace Now rally, demanding end to violence and resumption of negotiations; photo (Tel Aviv Journal) (M)

2001-08-09.title: World Briefing | Asia: India: Mental Homes Closed
2001-08-09.abstract: India's government orders closing of all 15 poorly constructed shelters that house mental patients in southern town of Erwadi, where fire recently killed 27 inmates in one facility (S)

2001-08-09.title: IRAN LEADER VOWS TO ENACT REFORMS IN HIS SECOND TERM
2001-08-09.abstract: Pres Mohammad Khatami, delivering inaugural address for his second term, vows to enact reforms clearly demanded by Iranian voters and warns formidable opponents that failure would reflect badly on very idea of Islamic government; Khatami garnered 77 percent of vote in June against nine mostly conservative opponents; Khatami, in theory second-most-powerful figure in country after supreme leader, Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, admonishes that hardening of factional positions risks alienating public from their faith; challenges he and his reformist allies face are in clear evidence; his inauguration itself was delayed for three days because of dispute between reformers and conservatives, and conservative judiciary shuts down another reformist newspaper; photo (M)

2001-08-09.title: In Jiang's Words: 'I Hope the Western World Can Understand China Better'
2001-08-09.abstract: Following are excerpts of an interview with Jiang Zemin conducted by The Times' publisher as well as senior editors and China-based correspondents

2001-08-09.title: Chinese President Expresses Optimism on Relations With U.S.
2001-08-09.abstract: In a rare, wide-ranging interview, President Jiang Zemin said that he believes George W. Bush is a leader he "could do business with.

2001-08-10.title: World Briefing | The Americas: Mexico: Bombs Outside Banks
2001-08-10.abstract: Three small bombs explode outside several Banamex bank branches in Mexico City in apparent protest against sale of Mexico's most powerful financial institutions to foreign companies (S)

2001-08-10.title: World Briefing | Asia: China: U.S. To Pay Expenses
2001-08-10.abstract: United States Government decides to pay China about $34,500 in compensation for expenses incurred during stand-off over Navy surveillance plane that made emergency landing on Hainan island; US rejects figure of $1 million that Chinese demanded for feeding and care of 24 crew members detained for 11 days (S)

2001-08-10.title: Israeli Aides Vow to Hold On to Seized Palestinian Offices
2001-08-10.abstract: As Israel draped itself in mourning for its latest terrorism victims, senior Israeli officials vowed today to keep control of important Palestinian offices seized overnight

2001-08-10.title: World Briefing | Middle East: Turkey: Broadcast Ban
2001-08-10.abstract: Turkish government's broadcasting watchdog orders Germany's Deutsche Welle and Britain's BBC to stop their Turkish-language radio broadcasts into country (S)

2001-08-10.title: Unrest Intensifies in Struggle to Control Zimbabwe Farms
2001-08-10.abstract: Tension flares across Zimbabwe as government vows to step up its seizures of white-owned farms and Western countries weigh punitive steps; Chinhoyi has become focal point of recent land disputes; recent clashes between white farmers and poor blacks who occupy farm lands 22 white men, all but one of them farmers, in jail charged with causing public violence; David Rockingham-Gill of Commercial Farmers Union says dozens of white families are fleeing their homesteads after confrontations with black squatters; Pres Robert Mugabe says white farmers have increased confrontations in ploy to win sympathy in United States and among Commonwealth nations; Mugabe's opponents say another wave of government-sanctioned intimidation is under way; photo; map (M)

2001-08-11.title: ISRAELIS GRIEVE, AND STRIKE BACK
2001-08-11.abstract: As Israel draped itself in mourning for its latest terrorism victims, senior Israeli officials vowed today to keep control of important Palestinian offices that the army and the police had seized overnight.    Hours after a Palestinian suicide bomber killed 15 people and himself on Thursday in a crowded pizzeria in downtown Jerusalem, Israel responded with a direct blow to the heart of Palestinian nationalism.

2001-08-11.title: National Briefing | South: Alabama: Hospital For Ex-Klansman
2001-08-11.abstract: Judge James Garrett of Birmingham, Ala, orders former Ku Klux Klansman Bobby Frank Cherry committed to state psychiatric hospital or further evaluation three weeks after declaring him mentally incompetent to stand trial in 1963 bombing of church which killed four black girls; photo (S)

2001-08-11.title: World Briefing | The Americas: Mexico: Acquittal Denounced
2001-08-11.abstract: Robert Rivard, editor of San Antonio Express-News, denounces acquittal of two Mexican suspects in murder of Philip True, correspondent for newspaper who was found dead after disappearing during 100-mile hike in state of Jalisco (S)

2001-08-11.title: National Briefing | Southwest: Texas: Twist In State Politics
2001-08-11.abstract: Lt Gov Bill Ratliff of Texas says he will run for re-election to State Senate; photo (S)

2001-08-11.title: Caucasus Abductions Raise Georgian Anxiety
2001-08-11.abstract: Everyone driving out of the international airport here encounters a startling billboard a few miles down the only access road.     ''Kidnapped'' is the message blaring in tall letters in English and Georgian above and below the photograph of a smiling man in a business shirt and tie.

2001-08-12.title: In Jerusalem, Making Targets of Each Other's Symbols
2001-08-12.abstract: Israelis and Palestinians target each other's symbols in continuing conflict, as neither side shows any sign of backing away; photo of Palestinian figure Hanan Ashrawi joining protest near Palestinian center, Orient House (M)

2001-08-12.title: New Rights for Dutch Prostitutes, but No Gain
2001-08-12.abstract: Netherlands, where prostitution has long been legal, has now legalized brothels and is trying to devise regulatory system; new law is intended to help police control industry and grant labor rights and protections to more than 30,000 working prostitutes, but many are now complaining about stupid bureaucratic rules and regulations that may force operations to close down or go underground; prostitutes trying to set themselves up as self-employed businesswomen are being turned down by accountants, banks and health insurance companies; photos; map (M)

2001-08-12.title: All Aboard, Vladivostok! A Dream Is Growing
2001-08-12.abstract: Renewed talk of railroading in far north Yukon territory includes proposal to build 900 miles of track between northern British Columbia and Fairbanks, Alaska; old rail town of Whitehorse also debates maps showing how rail link could arc from British Columbia to Siberia (M)

2001-08-12.title: Killing Touches a Nerve in Ex-Soviet Land
2001-08-12.abstract: Unsettling instability and mistrust in Georgia is brought to surface with murder of popular television anchor Giorgi Sanaia, for what are believed to be political reasons; photos; map; Pres Eduard A Shevardnadze, unlike counterparts in other former Soviet republics, does not rule with iron hand; criticism of government and protests tolerated in Georgia would be brutally suppressed elsewhere, but some in country believe that only harsh dictatorship would be able to crack down on deep and pervasive corruption (M)

2001-08-12.title: U.S. to Renew Relationship With Military In Indonesia
2001-08-12.abstract: Bush administration plans to renew some US ties to Indonesian military, most powerful institution in sprawling Southeast Asian country; aim is to cement ties after smooth ascent of new president, Megawati Sukarnoputri, who is backed by military (M)

2001-08-13.title: Bombing, and Shooting of Arab Girl Deepen Fear in Israel
2001-08-13.abstract: Struggle over shuttered Palestinian offices in Jerusalem intensifies with another Palestinian suicide bombing in cafe outside Haifa, wounding 15 people; eight-year-old Palestinian girl, Sabreen Abu Snainen, is fatally shot in head in exchange of gunfire between Israeli forces and Palestinian gunmen in Hebron; senior Palestinian official warns Palestinians will resort to political and armed resistance to oust Israeli forces from Palestinian institutions they have occupied since suicide bombing in Jerusalem pizzeria, which killed 15 people; photos (M)

2001-08-13.title: 20 Koreans Cut Off Fingers in an Anti-Japanese Protest
2001-08-13.abstract: Twenty young Korean protesters chopped off the tips of their little fingers today while shouting "Apologize, apologize!" for offenses that they said the Japanese had committed against the Korean people

2001-08-13.title: Political Leaders Sign Peace Accord in Macedonia
2001-08-13.abstract: Macedonia's two main ethnic groups signed a peace deal today to end six months of fighting, although hours later the sound of artillery fire rumbled from north of the capital

2001-08-13.title: Palestinians Hold Strike in Jerusalem
2001-08-13.abstract: Palestinians held a general strike today to protest Israel's takeover of their unofficial headquarters in Jerusalem

2001-08-13.title: Biden Criticizes White House On Korea Move
2001-08-13.abstract: Sen Joseph R Biden Jr, Senate Foreign Relations Committee chairman, criticizes Bush administration for adding conventional weapons to agenda of any future talks with North Korea; says if talks resume US should focus on 'first things first,' referring to potential of North Korea to build missiles capable of reaching as far as US; administration has put focus on conventional forces in response to intelligence reports that North Koreans have moved 80 perecnt of 1.1 million troops to within 50

2001-08-14.title: Macedonia Peace Signed, but Soon After, Artillery Booms
2001-08-14.abstract: Bitter and distrustful leaders of Macedonia's two main ethnic groups, Slavs and Albanians, sign peace accord to end six months of conflict but sporadic firefights flare between government forces and rebels north of capital of Skopje; deal puts Macedonia closer to deployment of 3,500 NATO troops to disarm ethnic Albanian guerrillas who launched insurgency six months ago for greater political and cultural rights; NATO secretary general Lord Robertson makes clear full disarmament mission depends on Macedonia's meeting several conditions, most importantly, 'sustainable' cease-fire; photos (M)

2001-08-14.title: World Briefing | Asia: Bangladesh: Pre-Election Violence
2001-08-14.abstract: Political violence rocks much of Bangladesh as campaigning heats up for national elections, which are expected in October (S)

2001-08-14.title: Japan's Premier Visits War Shrine, Pleasing Few
2001-08-14.abstract: Japan's Prime Min Junichoro Koizumi visits Yasukuni shrine near Tokyo that honors nation's war dead; yields to international pressure by abandoning plan to do so on August 15 anniversary of Japanese surrender in World War II; change of plan disappoints supporters and fails to mollify critics; he is met by small band of protestors, representing Korean residents in Japan and group of university students, who criticize decision to visit under any circumstances; original plan to visit on August 15 raised hackles of Asian neighbors, particularly South Korea and China; Asian countries regard Yasukuni, which includes war criminals among 2.47 million Japanese soldiers honored there, as symbol of Japanese militarism; photos (M)

2001-08-14.title: World Briefing | Africa: Zimbabwe: 12 Charged In Farm Violence
2001-08-14.abstract: Zimbabwe government announces arrests of 12 men charged in connection with violence around Chinhoyi, where white farmers are fleeing to avoid mobs of militants who invade farms and burn and loot homes; authorities say farmers are exaggerating extent of violence (S)

2001-08-14.title: I.R.A. Retracts Arms Offer, Bringing Peace Effort to Halt
2001-08-14.abstract: Just two days after Britain said a lasting peace was "tantalizingly close," the Irish Republican Army withdrew an offer to dispose of its weapons

2001-08-15.title: 20 Arrested in Protests at Genoa Are Released and Deported
2001-08-15.abstract: Twenty people who spent last three weeks in Italian jails after protesting at recent summit meeting of major industrialized nations in Genoa are released and immediately deported from Italy; American Susanna Thomas is among those released (M)

2001-08-15.title: Israeli Officials Warn of More Raids
2001-08-15.abstract: Israel kept tanks poised outside Palestinian towns today, with Israeli officials warning that they would strike if Palestinians started shooting again

2001-08-15.title: Zimbabwe Seizes 4 Journalists for Linking Police to Looters
World Briefing | Asia: South Korea: Group To Attend Northern Festivities
2001-08-15.abstract: South Korean government reverses itself and decides to allow 360 civic and religious activists to go to North Korea for celebration marking 56th anniversary of end of Japanese colonial rule over Korean Peninsula (S)

2001-08-15.title: Colombia Arrests 3 as I.R.A. Bomb Experts
2001-08-15.abstract: Three men suspected of being members of Irish Republican Army are arrested in Colombia on charges of having provided training in explosives to Revolutionary Armed Forces of Colombia; development raises doubts in Northern Ireland about IRA's commitment to peace efforts and renews concerns that Colombian guerrillas are planning urban terrorist attacks; two suspects are identified as James Monaghan and Martin McCaulley and third used alias David Bracken; photo (M)

2001-08-16.title: Forceful U.S. Effort On Mideast Is Urged
2001-08-16.abstract: Egypt's top foreign policy official warned that the Bush administration's ''inaction'' in the Middle East could allow extremists to take hold in moderate Arab countries. This would be a consequence far graver than the administration's apparent fear of risking its prestige with an Israeli-Palestinian peace initiative, said Osama el-Baz, President Hosni Mubarak's national security adviser.    In addition to its effect on Arab states, Mr. Baz said in an interview, the United States' approach in the region was helping to weaken the forces of moderation in Israel, where many people were hoping that the Bush administration would take what he called ''a clear-cut position to check the mad spiral of violence.''

2001-08-16.title: Zimbabwe Arrests 4 Over News About Police
2001-08-16.abstract: Authorities in Zimbabwe arrest three senior editors and one reporter from nation's only independent daily newspaper, The Daily News, one day after paper reports that militants used police cars during recent looting of white-owned farms; journalists are charged with publishing 'false news' and are eventually released but warned they may be called in for further questioning; arrests mark Zimbabwean government's latest effort to stifle independent news media, which have sharply criticized authorities for condoning political violence, disregarding its courts and encouraging invasion of white-owned farms (M)

2001-08-16.title: Ethnic Albanian Leader Pledges to Disarm
2001-08-16.abstract: Ali Ahmeti, a political leader of the ethnic Albanian rebels, pledged Thursday that his troops would disarm once NATO forces arrived in Macedonia

2001-08-16.title: Added Rights For Indians In Mexico Take Effect
2001-08-16.abstract: Law originally intended to expand rights of 10 million Indians in Mexico takes effect, but Congress waters down law so much that it pleases few people and infuriates many; law was at first intended to settle Zapatista uprising in Chiapas and grant measure of legal and social autonomy to indigenous people; lawmakers erase most of clauses that call for Indian autonomy over land and natural resources, leaving only general ban against racism (M)

2001-08-16.title: Europe's Ties With America:New Cause For Concern
2001-08-16.abstract: "The problem with allies," Winston Churchill said, "is that they sometimes develop opinions of their own."

2001-08-17.title: World Briefing
2001-08-17.abstract: ASIA AFGHANISTAN: ENVOYS ARE REBUFFED Three envoys — an American, an Australian and a German — met with Abdul Rehman Ottaq, left, director of the Taliban consulate department, but were refused visits with the eight aid workers held on charges of preaching Christianity. The envoys gave the Taliban letters for the workers and bags of cookies and supplies. Under Taliban law, a foreigner found guilty of preaching Christianity would face a short jail term and expulsion. Mr. Ottaq said the investigation has broadened to include all aid groups, including the United Nations and the World Food Program.    (AP) NORTH KOREA: PEACE GROUP ALLEGES PRESSURE A spokesman for a delegation of 311 South Korean peace activists, Lee Soo Un, said that North Korean officials had pressured them to attend ceremonies at a new monument in Pyongyang dedicated to Korean reunification on terms not acceptable to the South. A third of the group attended the opening ceremony on Wednesday, the 56th anniversary of the end of Japanese colonial rule on the Korean Peninsula, according to pooled South Korean newspaper reports. South Korean officials ordered those going to Pyongyang for the anniversary not to appear to endorse the North's position by visiting the monument.    Don Kirk (NYT) INDIA: CAMPAIGN FOR SAINTHOOD A 35,000-page report on the life of Mother Teresa, put together by a diocesan commission in Calcutta, will be sent to the Vatican in six sealed boxes. Her order, the Missionaries of Charity, hopes she will be beatified and eventually be made a saint

2001-08-17.title: NATO Puts Off Its Decision on Sending Full Macedonia Force
2001-08-17.abstract: NATO, waiting for a report by commanders, today put off until next week a decision on whether to send a 3,500-member force to Macedonia

2001-08-17.title: Megawati Outlines a Cure for Indonesia's Ills
2001-08-17.abstract: Megawati Sukarnoputri, in first major speech as president of Indonesia, presents outline of cure for country's many problems, stressing stability, human rights, fiscal responsibility and battle against corruption; surprises her critics by taking workmanlike approach that addresses several misgivings people have had about her; she has impressed analysts with her steady and commanding approach and with beginnings of surprisingly liberal program that appears to allay fears that she might be puppet of military and roll back reforms of recent years; most notably, she has apologized for human rights abuses committed by military in combating two separate rebellions and calls on armed forces to reform itself (M)

2001-08-17.title: Article 2001081792818054177 -- No Title
2001-08-17.abstract: BEIJING, Aug. 17 -- A Beijing court convicted four people of murder today for organizing the self-immolation last January by followers of the outlawed Falun Gong spiritual movement. One man was sentenced to life in prison and the others received terms ranging from seven to 15 years.

2001-08-17.title: Newspapers Accuse Kim of Political Move : Seoul Seeks Warrants Against Press Magnates
2001-08-17.abstract: SEOUL—  Five South Korean newspaper owners and executives faced arrest Thursday after prosecutors sought warrants for them on charges of large-scale tax evasion and embezzlement. The move escalated a long-running struggle between President Kim Dae Jung and South Korea's news organizations.

2001-08-18.title: World Briefing | Asia: Cambodia: Khmer Rouge Apology
Egypt Seeking U.S. Monitors To Help End Mideast Violence
2001-08-18.abstract: Egyptian envoy Osama el-Baz urges Bush administration to take more active role in Mideast and to weigh sending American monitors to defuse Israeli-Palestinian violence, meetings with Sec Colin L Powell and national security adviser Condoleezza Rice, Washington; seeks to salvage failed cease-fire in region; photo (M)

2001-08-18.title: World Briefing | Asia: Brunei: Here Come The Creditors
Once a Savior, It Now Saps Region's Recovery : China's Growth Weighs On Neighboring Countries
2001-08-18.abstract: SINGAPORE—  CORRECTION PUBLISHED IN THE INTERNATIONAL HERALD TRIBUNE DATED August 24, 2001:An article Saturday about the effect of China's economic strength on its Asian neighbors overstated the amount of production that ON Semiconductor Corp. will shift from Malaysia to China. The move will involve only some of ON's operations in Malaysia.

2001-08-18.title: U.S. Plane's Odd Trip From China
2001-08-18.abstract: Navy surveillance plane that collided with Chinese jet fighter was hauled away from Hainan Island, China, by Lockheed Martin in belly of commercial Russian cargo plane, largest in world; United States Air Force describes June 16-July 3 recovery operation, in which plane was cut into pieces; photos (M)

2001-08-19.title: Trees Bind Angkor Temples in Perilous Grip
2001-08-19.abstract: Trees threaten massive temples of Angkor, Cambodia, one of world's great cities between 9th and 15th centures; temples are one of history's architectural glories, but roots of century-old trees are have dug their way so deeply into monuments that they hold them at their mercy, binding them even as they rip them apart; photos; map (L)

2001-08-19.title: Zimbabwe Fund for AIDS Patients Is Frozen in Bureaucracy
2001-08-19.abstract: Zimbabwe government for more than year has been collecting 3 percent tax on personal and corporate income to pay for national AIDS trust, to help care for country's many AIDS victims, but most of millions raised so far is unspent, mired in bureaucracy that has frustrated many people who welcomed trust's creation (M)

2001-08-19.title: Rice on Front Lines As Adviser to Bush
2001-08-19.abstract: Condoleezza Rice, the president's national security adviser, came to Washington signaling that she would be a behind-the-scenes broker among the competing views that shape foreign policy.     Instead, she has turned out to be a very active and very public foreign policy maker, administration officials and outside experts say.

2001-08-19.title: Rice on Front Line in Foreign Policy Role
2001-08-19.abstract: Bush administration officials and outside experts say that Condoleeza Rice has quickly amassed power and turned out to be very active and very public foreign policy maker; Rice, and not State Sec Colin Powell, was first Bush foreign policy official to visit Moscow; Rice and Powell have played surprising, and contrasting, roles in administration; administration officials say that Rice's ascendancy comes as she has aligned herself with more conservative members of Pres Bush's foreign policy team, leaving State Dept feeling 'outnumbered'; photo (M)

2001-08-19.title: Slow to Yield, Brazil Passes Equal Rights For Its Women
2001-08-19.abstract: Brazil, after 26 years of debate, amendments, delays and parliamentary maneuvering, approves legal code that for first time in country's history makes women equal to men in eyes of law; among oddities new measure eliminates is much-criticized provision that allows husband to obtain annulment if he learns that his wife was not virgin at time of their marriage; new code also does away with restriction that prevents anyone determined to have engaged in adultery, which is criminal offense in Brazil, from remarrying (M)

2001-08-20.title: Khmer Rouge Suspects Feel Pressure Is Building for Trial
2001-08-20.abstract: In a sure sign that the trials of former Khmer Rouge leaders in Cambodia are really coming, the accused are all preparing their defenses

2001-08-20.title: WORLD'S ECONOMY SLOWS TO A WALK IN RARE LOCK STEP
2001-08-20.abstract: World economy, which grew at raging pace in 2000, has slowed to crawl as US, Europe, Japan and some major developing countries undergo rare simultaneous slump; latest economic statistics show many regional economic powers have become economically stagnant, defying expectations that growth in other countries would help compensate for slowdown in US; many experts say that though $33 trillion world economy is still likely to expand in 2001, but that world is experiencing economic whiplash, with growth rates retreating more quickly than at any time since oil shock of 1973; there is no single factor to account for widespread weakness, persuading economists that recovery may be slow in coming; biggest surprise is sluggish performance in Europe, especially Germany, where leaders had until recently, thought they could escape American slowdown; result is that Europe, with combined economy about as big as US, is in no position to take over as locomotive of world economic growth; photos; charts (L)

2001-08-20.title: Manila Peace Bid Paying Off in Muslim Area
2001-08-20.abstract: COTABATO CITY, Philippines—  The low-level civil war that has raged here on the sprawling and impoverished island of Mindanao intermittently for three decades has dragged down the Philippine economy, drained the government's budget and provided a major obstacle in forging closer ties among Southeast Asian countries.

2001-08-20.title: Donald Woods, 67, Editor and Apartheid Foe
2001-08-20.abstract: Donald Woods, crusading white newspaper editor who championed black anti-apartheid activist Steve Biko, dies at age 67; photo (M)

2001-08-20.title: Doctors' Dirty Needles Spreading Disease in China
2001-08-20.abstract: China's heavy use of injections and infusions is becoming medical nightmare, particularly in rural parts of country, where syringes and needles are often inadequately sterilized and are contributing to alarming spread of blood-borne infections, particularly hepatitis and, to lesser extent, AIDS; 60 percent of Chinese have had hepatitis B, compared with just 1 percent in US and Japan; estimated 150 million Chinese have chronic variety of infection; doctors in rural areas often have little formal medical training and receive extra income for each injection they give, and patients and doctors alike see shots as sign of progress; map; photo (M)

2001-08-21.title: World Briefing | Africa: Sierra Leone: Fighters Turn In Guns
2001-08-21.abstract: Rebels and pro-government militiamen begin turn

2001-08-21.title: World Briefing | Asia: India: Dispute Over Christians
2001-08-21.abstract: Christian leaders and opposition Congress Party score Indian Prime Min Atal Behari Vajpayee for saying some missionaries improperly offer welfare services to poor in hopes of converting them; say remarks will worsen insecurity of nation's tiny Christian minority (S)0

2001-08-21.title: For Palestinians, a Daily, Dirty Obstacle Course
2001-08-21.abstract: Israeli roadblocks sprinkled across West Bank and Gaza have become defining physical feature of conflict that has consumed both sides for past 11 months; have trapped thousands of Palestinians in their towns and divided both areas into virtual cantons; for Israel blockades are essential security measure, but for Palestinians they are source of daily hardship and humiliation; maps; photos (M)

2001-08-21.title: F.B.I.'s Inquiry In Cole Attack Is Nearing Halt
2001-08-21.abstract: FBI investigation of bombing of US destroyer Cole in port of Aden, Yemen, in October 2001 that killed 17 American sailors virtually grinds to halt; agency cites refusal by Yemen to heed US requests to broaden inquiry to include Islamic militant groups; says government's decision has frustrated efforts to link bombing conclusively to Osama bin Laden, fugitive Saudi, who has declared worldwide holy war against US; says Yemen has denied it access to prominent Yemenis it wishes to interview in bid to link attack to elements of bin Laden's network in Yemen, which became key base for him in early 1990's; photo (M)

2001-08-21.title: World Briefing | Asia: Japan: Dispute On Islands Flares
2001-08-21.abstract: Japan protests to Russia granting of fishing rights to foreign boats in waters around Kurile Islands, claimed by Japan but held by Russia since end of World War II (S)

2001-08-22.title: New Middle East Peace Bid Brokered by German Envoy
2001-08-22.abstract: Relations between Israel and Palestinians have so thoroughly deteriorated that glimmer of diplomatic action, planned meeting organized by German Min Joschka Fischer, is seized on as possible path to truce; small bomb explodes in Russian Compound section of Jerusalem, punctuating difficulties; photos; no time or place is set for Min Shimon Peres to meet with Yasir Arafat (M)

2001-08-22.title: World Briefing | Asia: Indonesia: New Leader Hits The Road
2001-08-22.abstract: Pres Megawati Sukarnoputri of Indonesia departs on visit to Philippines; photo (S)

2001-08-22.title: Argentina Gets $8 Billion Aid From the I.M.F.
2001-08-22.abstract: IMF will provide up to $8 billion in emergency aid to Argentina to stabilize its economy; agreement follows protracted talks between Argentina and Bush administration, which had sought show skepticism about bailouts like those approved by Clinton administration; package provides incentives for Argentina to reach agreement with creditors on restructuring $128 billion foreign debt; plan could pressure US banks and other lenders to take losses; Sec Paul O'Neill has used case to show administration will support bailouts only when nation takes painful actions before seeking help; photo (M)

2001-08-22.title: Fred Hoyle Dies at 86; Opposed 'Big Bang' but Named It
2001-08-22.abstract: Sir Fred Hoyle, one of most creative and provocative astrophysicists of last half century, who helped explain how heavier elements were formed and gave name Big Bang, meant to be derisive, to theory of cosmic origin he vehemently opposed, dies at age 86; photo (M)

2001-08-22.title: World Briefing | Europe: Russia: Chechnya Investigations
2001-08-22.abstract: Chechnya's civilian prosecutor reports completing investigation of 48 cases of crimes committed by Russian troops, but many cases await action by military prosecutors; human rights groups complain about dual system of justice, where civilian investigators are denied access to military witnesses and evidence (S)

2001-08-23.title: World Briefing | Asia: Bangladesh: Election In October
2001-08-23.abstract: Bangladesh will hold parliamentary election on Oct 1 (S)

2001-08-23.title: Barretos Journal; Brazil's Wild and Woolly Side: Meet the Caubóis
2001-08-23.abstract: Brazil is experiencing full-fledged rodeo boom; rich ranchers and humble farm hands alike have embraced sport; in Barretos, where modest rodeo was first held in 1956, competition takes place at 35,000-seat arena with purses of up to $20,000, attracting competitors from as far away as United States, Canada and Australia; more than million people attend 11-day event each year; photos; map (M)

2001-08-23.title: World Briefing | Europe: Yugoslavia: New Charges For Milosevic Aide
2001-08-23.abstract: Prosecutors in Yugoslavia charge Radomir Markovic, Slobodan Milosevic's secret police chief, with four counts of murder for organizing highway crash that killed four members of opposition party in 1999; photo (S)

2001-08-23.title: Religious Arrests Cast a Pall Over Afghanistan Aid Efforts
2001-08-23.abstract: Afghanistan's ruling Taliban has uneasy relationship with foreign aid organizations and workers in their poverty-stricken country; there are cultural conflicts and mutual distrust, as evidenced by arrest two weeks ago of 24 aid workers, accused of preaching Christian Gospel to Afghans; Abdul Ghafoor Afghani, Taliban's chief of protocol, claims those arrested were given visas as aid workers, not missionaries; Taliban is uncomfortable with what goes on behind high walls of aid workers' compounds: mingling of unmarried men and women, beat of rock 'n' roll, drinking at end of day; repeatedly, Taliban have warned against turning Afghan believers into Afghan apostates; arrested aid workers belong to Shelter Now, German-based group, but Foreign Min Wakil Ahmed Mutawakil has said that World Food Program itself is prime suspect; potential of more arrests leaves aid agencies in fear; huge withdrawal--or expulsion--of agencies would be catastrophic for needy; photos; map (M)

2001-08-23.title: Despite Aid, Argentina Will See More Pain
2001-08-23.abstract: Argentine government tells population that even deeper cuts in spending and more national sacrifice are in offing, despite emergency rescue agreement with International Monetary Fund; Pres Fernando de la Rua tells country's 36 million people that they should be happy with new accord, but reaction is mixed; Argentine press had been talking, perhaps unrealistically, of much larger lifeline than IMF has agreed to after two weeks of tough negotiations; timing of agreement will be bad for politicians; congressional elections are scheduled for Oct 14, with people irate at travails they are being asked to endure and eager to punish those they view as responsible; graph (M)

2001-08-24.title: Bush Declares U.S. Will Quit ABM Pact
2001-08-24.abstract: President Bush said flatly that the United States would withdraw from the 1972 Antiballistic Missile Treaty ''at a time convenient to America.'' But he insisted that he had ''no specific timetable'' for when he would scrap the treaty in order to begin testing a missile defense system.

2001-08-24.title: CHINA NOW FACING AN AIDS EPIDEMIC, A TOP AIDE ADMITS
2001-08-24.abstract: Senior Chinese official openly admits that China is facing AIDS epidemic that threatens to outpace government efforts to control it; Deputy Health Min Yin Dakui, in surprise news conference, departs from Chinese government's general reticence on subject of AIDS; concedes government's failure to develop effective education programs and tendency of some local officials to cover up extent of infection in their jurisdictions, allowing disease to spread unchecked; Yin discusses, for first time, AIDS epidemic covered up in Henan Province, where tens of thousands of poor farmers contracted AIDS by selling their blood using unsterilized practices; government's apparent commitment to greater openness about AIDS is undercut by fact that reports on Yin's lengthy news conference in state-controlled press are brief and statistical, leaving out Yin's judgment that China faces 'serious epidemic' and not mentioning Henan Province by name at all; photos (M)

2001-08-24.title: Partners Who Felled a Giant Now Feud Over Serbia's Path
2001-08-24.abstract: Serbian Pres Vojislav Kostunica and Prime Min Zoran Djindjic, partners in 18-party governing alliance that toppled Slobodan Milosevic, are now bitter rivals; Kostunica has styled himself as guardian of traditional values and of Yugoslav federation; Djindjic sees himself as modernizer and economic reformer; differences recently burst into open with Kostunica's party announcing its withdrawal from Djindjic's government; two are expected to patch up their feud, but dispute bodes ill for smooth running of government that badly needs foreign aid to help bolster its credibility with Serbia's impoverished people (M)

2001-08-24.title: World Briefing | Asia: Sri Lanka: Rebels Continue Attacks

2001-08-25.title: Jews in Hebron Only Wish That the Army Would Stay
2001-08-25.abstract: Israeli forces go deep into Palestinian-ruled sectors of Hebron, taking control for a while of hilltop neighborhood that has been used for months by Palestinian gunmen to fire on small community of Jewish settlers; settlers say they wish army would stay; photo (M)

2001-08-25.title: Efforts to Muzzle the Press Spread in Southern Africa
2001-08-25.abstract: African journalists fear other countries in southern Africa are emulting Zimbabwe's muzzling of press; Zambia's shutdown of radio station and jailing of newspaper editor Fred M'membe cited, as well as pressure from officials even in relatively placid places like Botswana and Namibia; photo of Geoff Nyarota, journalist arrested recently in Zimbabwe (M)

2001-08-25.title: Bush Criticizes Arafat on Terrorism
2001-08-25.abstract: Pres Bush issues one of his harshest critiques yet of Yasir Arafat, saying he must 'put 100 percent effort into solving the terrorist activity' before peace talks can begin (S)

2001-08-25.title: East German Files On Berlin Leaders Cast Shadows Still
2001-08-25.abstract: Intelligence files of Stasi secret service in former East Germany continue to haunt politics in new Germany; Marianne Birthler, Stasi archives director who is charged with opening files as much as possible, wants to release material that might shed light on former Chancelor Helmut Kohl's use of political slush funds, which he refuses to discuss; Kohl has gone to court, backed by Birthler's boss, Interior Min Otto Schily; Schily was leftist civil rights lawyer who in this case agrees with Kohl that victims of illegal spying have right to keep such material private (M)

2001-08-25.title: The Iron Lady's Sequel Haunts Britain's Tories
2001-08-25.abstract: Margaret Thatcher, still steely and crusty at age 75, continues to insert herself into British politics, campaigning for Conservative candidates in last two elections, undeterred by defeats; photo; former prime minister's latest foray is endorsement of Iain Duncan Smith for party leader and denunciation of rival Kenneth Clarke (M)

2001-08-26.title: Taliban Will Allow Access to Jailed Christian Aid Workers
2001-08-26.abstract: Co-signed 'confession' of two American aid workers whom Taliban claim to have caught foisting their Christian faith on Afghan Muslims is displayed by Taliban's religious police in Kabul detention center; Dayna Curry and Heather Mercer say they gave material about Jesus to one family; they and two Australians and four Germans, all working with German-based group Shelter Now, are in jail in Kabul for last three weeks; 16 Afghans also are under arrest; Taliban has refused access to foreigners, but now says it will allow Red Cross to visit them; Taliban has yet to announce any timetable for legal proceedings against aid workers, although officials say all of them may be subject to death penalty; photos; map (M)

2001-08-26.title: Palestinian Raids Kill 6 Israelis, Including 3 Soldiers at Gaza Base
2001-08-26.abstract: Palestinians stage boldest and deadliest raid on Israeli Army base in 11-month-old conflict, killing six Israelis, including three soldiers shot in Gaza Strip; dead civilians are from nearby Israeli settlement, shot while riding in car along road that has become frequent target for gunmen; two children in car are less severely wounded; Israel retaliates swiftly, sending jets to fire missiles at Palestinian police headquarters in Gaza City, wounding three Palestinian policemen and causing heavy damage; Israeli tanks receive heavy machine gunfire from Palestinians as they move deeply into Palestinian-controlled areas near Rafah, at southern tip of Gaza Strip; Israeli fighter planes are also heard over Ramallah; group called Al Aksa Martyrs Brigade, allied with Yasir Arafat's Fatah movement, takes responsibility for shooting of settler family; attack on soldiers in Gaza is claimed by Democratic Front for the Liberation of Palestine, radical group that is at odds with Arafat; Israeli officials put onus for both attacks entirely on Arafat and his Palestinian Authority; photos; map (M)

2001-08-26.title: Uncommon Royal Couple Exchange Vows in Norway
2001-08-26.abstract: Crown Prince Haakon, heir to Norwegian throne, exchanges marriage vows with Mette-Marit Tjessem Hoiby, former waitress with 4-year-old son from previous relationship and admitted history of heavy partying in Oslo's drug-filled 'house party' mileau; pomp and splendor of ceremony is unusual for egalitarian Nordic kingdom; support for monarchy as institution hit record lows after couple moved in together, but 62 percent of Norwegians still call themselves monarchists; photo (M)

2001-08-26.title: Argentina's Stopgap Cash Gets Some Funny Looks
2001-08-26.abstract: Argentina has secured $8 billion credit line from International Monetary Fund, but its finances continue to be chaotic; peso is tied to American dollar at one-to-one rate; Central Bank is unable to print new pesos unless it has dollars to back them, leaving local governments squeezed to point that they cannot meet their financial obligations; province of Buenos Aires has begun paying its suppliers and employees and retirees with what it calls 'treasury letter in cancellation of obligations,' bond redeemable at 7 percent interest next year; scrip has been renamed with slang term meaning wampum; businesses that sell basic goods feel they are forced to accept so-called patacones, despite their misgivings; high-end shops and boutiques do not; for central government, immediate risk is that it might lose control of economic policy as nearly half of country's provinces consider issuing bonds; photos (M)

2001-08-26.title: Leisure Class to Working Class in Saudi Arabia
2001-08-26.abstract: Article in series, The Perils of Plenty, examines situation in Saudi Arabia, where virtually everyone was lifted out of poverty with oil revenues, but now petrodollars do not stretch as far and many incredulous Saudis confront fact that they will have to work at real jobs; baby boom generation spawned by 1970's oil wealth is wading into job market that can only generate one job for every two men; country must find ways to relax its rigid traditions to make economy grow; it needs jobs for new generation whose parents did not have to work; future may hinge on unresolved question of how to pass down absolute monarchy once all 44 sons of founder, King Abdel Azis al-Saud, are gone; photos; map. graphs (L)

2001-08-27.title: Death and Revenge Continue in Mideast
2001-08-27.abstract: Hours after an Israeli man from the coastal town of Netanya was shot to death on a little-traveled road north of Tulkarm in the West Bank, the Israeli Army launched reprisal assaults, again bombarding police buildings and highway checkpoints. This time the targets were in Tulkarm and El Bireh, just north of Jerusalem. A Palestinian security officer was reportedly killed.    Article, Page A3.

2001-08-27.title: New Television Show Expected to Boost Opposition in Iraq
2001-08-27.abstract: Iraqi opposition groups will open a new front in their efforts to topple Saddam Hussein when a U.S.-funded satellite television program goes on the air in a few days

2001-08-27.title: Lau Journal; An Outpatient Exorcism (It Was Only a Crab)
2001-08-27.abstract: Sukari Yasir, dukun, or faith healer, in Lau, Indonesia, performs white magic to extract cursed objects that black magicians have inserted into people'e bodies to make them sick; photos; map (M)

2001-08-27.title: Achilles' Heel In Missile Plan: Crude Weapons
2001-08-27.abstract: Federal and private experts say missile defense system planned by Bush administration may be least able to destroy warheads from rogue countries thought to pose biggest threat; nations like North Korea, Iran and Iraq would fire wobbling, rudimentary warheads during attack, which are among hardest to hit; stopping rudimentary missile would be far harder than hitting advanced warheads, which are made to spin like tops or footballs to increase accuracy; crude warhead fired by inexperienced attacker is likely to have no spin stabilization and to gyrate wildly, tumbling end over end and making it harder to track; diagram (M)

2001-08-27.title: U.S. READY TO END SANCTIONS ON INDIA TO BUILD ALLIANCE
2001-08-27.abstract: Bush administration moves on broad front to strengthen relations with India, nation it views as neglected and potentially important strategic ally and trading partner in Asia; is almost certain to ask Congress to lift economic and military sanctions imposed in 1998 for its test of nuclear weapon; some of most senior legislators, including Senate Foreign Relations Committee chairman, Sen Joseph R Biden Jr, back move; lifting of sanctions would remove significant irritant to closer ties, and would also signal that US has little choice but to accept India's membership in nuclear club; administration is likely to ask Congress to lift some sanctions against neighboring Pakistan, which detonated own nuclear weapon in tit-for-tat test; Sen Biden says he is not prepared to support request for Pakistan, which has since come under military rule (M)

2001-08-28.title: Iraqi Opposition Ready to Broadcast Satellite TV Program Financed by U.S.
2001-08-28.abstract: Iraqi opposition groups will open new front in battle to topple Saddam Hussein by beaming satellite television program financed by US throughout region; program will include news, political profiles, call-in shows and music videos; channel, under logo Liberty TV, will operate from London; official Baghdad news media reports US spy plane was shot down over southern Iraq; Pentagon confirms loss of unmanned drone, which it says could have crashed because of mechanical failure (M)

2001-08-28.title: World Briefing | Europe: Spain: Big Bomb At Airport
2001-08-28.abstract: Huge car bomb explodes in parking garage at Madrid airport, causing extensive damage but no injuries; Basque separatist group ETA takes responsibility; photo (S)

2001-08-28.title: U.S. SAYS KILLINGS BY ISRAEL INFLAME MIDEAST CONFLICT
2001-08-28.abstract: Bush administration says Israel's policy of pinpoint killings of Palestinians is inflaming Middle East conflict and urges Israelis to alleviate what it calls humiliations of Palestinian people; reiterates appeal to Palestinian Authority to take sustained and credible steps to arrest those responsible for terrorism, but also appeals to Israel to soften policy toward Palestinians; tone is sterner following Israeli missile attack on offices of Mustafa Zubari, leader of radical Palestinian faction, which killed him and set off protests by thousands of Palestinians; photo (M)

2001-08-28.title: World Briefing | Europe: Ireland: Feeling Good
2001-08-28.abstract: Economic and Social Research Institute survey finds that more Irish people report a sense of well-being and good health than any other European nationality, with 80 percent saying their health is good or very good; higher income and education levels contribute to greater sense of well-being (S)

2001-08-28.title: Macedonia Disarming Begins, Hours After Briton's Death
2001-08-28.abstract: NATO peacekeeping troops arrive in Macedonian village of Otlja, and begin disarming ethnic Albanian rebels; soldiers from French Foreign Legion and British paratroopers arrive by helicopter; collect about 400 weapons for destruction, first such surrender under 30-day disarmament plan to reduce tensions and end conflict that threatens to spiral into fifth Balkan civil war in decade; British soldier dies of head injuries after gang of Macedonian youths hurl chunk of concrete through windshield of military jeep; map; photo (M)

2001-08-29.title: Israeli Army Captures Buildings In Palestinian West Bank Town
2001-08-29.abstract: Israeli forces take control of parts of Palestinian town of Beit Jala to stop snipers who have been firing for months on Gilo, neighboring Israeli area on southern edge of Jerusalem; Palestinians charge 'reoccupation' of area supposedly under their control; soldiers also move into Lutheran church compound, using top floors to shoot at gunmen who continue firing on Gilo and on Israeli troops; thousands of Palestinians vow revenge for killing of Mustafa Zibri (Abu Ali Mstafa), leader in Popular Front for Liberation of Palestine and PLO who Israel says was sponsor of car bombings and other terrorist attacks; US warns against raising stakes in conflict; Israeli officials say they have no intention of remaining in Beit Jala but will stay put until there is no more shooting on Gilo; Gen Gershon Yitzhak points out that Palestinian Authority 'did not do its job' in Beit Jala, predominantly Christian town of about 10,000 people; photos; map (M)

2001-08-29.title: World Briefing | Asia: Indonesia: New President's Plight
2001-08-29.abstract: Pres Megawati Sukarnoputri of Indonesia, visiting Malaysia, says she is ashamed to have to beg for economic aid from neighboring countries (S)

2001-08-29.title: World Briefing | Asia: Cambodia: Women's Aid Leader Honored
2001-08-29.abstract: Chanthol Oung, 34, who leads women's aid group called Cambodian Women's Crisis Center, becomes first recipient of new annual Ramon Maysaysay Award for emergent leadership (S)

2001-08-29.title: Graves of the Missing Haunt Sri Lanka
2001-08-29.abstract: Since the late 1980's, more than 20,000 people have vanished after being taken into government custody during two violent rebellions.

2001-08-29.title: U.S. to Attend, Warily, a U.N. Conference on Children's Rights
2001-08-29.abstract: Bush administration is engaged in strenuous effort to influence agenda of UN conference in September on rights of children, so that final declaration does not appear to lend support for abortion counseling; boycott is not being considered; Clinton administration approved agenda's phrase 'reproductive health rights,' which Bush administration, some Muslim countries and Vatican see as subtle endorsement of abortion (M)

2001-08-30.title: Milosevic to Face Charge of Genocide
2001-08-30.abstract: The chief prosecutor in the Hague war crimes trial of ex-Yugoslav leader Slobodan Milosevic said today she would charge him with genocide

2001-08-30.title: Euro Deposes the Mark, Grieving Many Germans
2001-08-30.abstract: Many in Germany who will have to give up their marks in three months do not trust the new currency.

2001-08-30.title: Iran and Azerbaijan Argue Over Caspian's Riches
2001-08-30.abstract: Iran has focused new attention on simmering dispute over how to carve up oil resources of Caspian Sea among five nations bordering inland sea: Azerbaijan, Iran, Kazakhstan, Russia and Turkmenistan; Iran is arguing for greater share than former Soviet republics are willing to give, and recently which sent warship to order Azerbaijan research vessel to stop exploring for oil in Caspian Sea; Azerbaijan's allies in West, including United States, called Iranian action provocative and neighboring Turkey dispatched fighter jets; outcome will help determine role played by Western governments and companies in developing and transporting as much as 200 billion barrels of oil and 600 billion cubic meters of gas estimated to be in Caspian; photo; map (M)

2001-08-30.title: Palestinians Reclaim West Bank Town After Israelis Withdraw
2001-08-30.abstract: Exhausted but exultant, Palestinians reclaimed neighborhoods today in Beit Jala, the town that had been taken over for more than two days by Israeli troops

2001-08-30.title: Herbert Haag, 86, Priest Who Challenged Vatican
2001-08-30.abstract: Herbert Haag, Swiss liberal Roman Catholic theologian who sought reform and liberalization of church, dies at age 86 (M)

2001-08-31.title: MILOSEVIC TO FACE CHARGES COVERING 3 WARS IN BALKANS
2001-08-31.abstract: Chief prosecutor of United Nations tribunal in Hague, Carla Del Ponte, says she will expand charges against Slobodan Milosevic to include genocide in massacres in Bosnia and accusations of war crimes in Croatia; says there will be one trial for three wars; tens of thousands of people were killed by Serbian forces in Bosnian war of 1992-95 as wide swaths of country were 'cleansed' of Muslim population; although pattern of killing in Croatia, Bosnia and into Kosovo was largely same, Milosevic has denied any culpability; by including genocide charge in new indictments, Del Ponte will dramatically extend scope of former president's trial; Milosevic appears in court for second time, sniping at judges and calling them tools of Western governments, and complaining about being isolated from press; photo (M)

2001-08-31.title: Govan Mbeki, 91, an Enemy Of Apartheid System, Dies
2001-08-31.abstract: Govan Mbeki, South African liberation leader and father of Pres Thabo Mbeki, dies at age 91; photo (M)

2001-08-31.title: On Tourism, Mexico Now Thinks Green
2001-08-31.abstract: Mexico's Environmental Min Victor Lichtinger is cracking down on resort development and has closed down or suspended 19 projects; but battle is tug of war, as Pres Vicente Fox and National Tourism Development Fund chief John McCarthy have at least three tourism megaprojects in mind; one runs from Nuevo Vallarta into Nayarit state on Pacific coast, another along southern Caribbean coast and third along Gulf of California coast; map; photo (M)

2001-08-31.title: Yes, Yes, It's the Euro! No, No, Don't Touch!
2001-08-31.abstract: European Central Bank is so concerned about giving counterfeiters head start that it has prohibited almost anybody from getting too good a look at real euro bills or coins; at same time, it is kicking off continentwide advertising campaign to spur acceptance of mystery-shrouded currency, which is to go into general circulation on Jan 1; anti-counterfeiting features described (M)

2001-08-31.title: Sign-Ups for Foreign Service Test Nearly Double After 10-Year Ebb
2001-08-31.abstract: State Department announces that nearly twice as many people have registered to take Foreign Service exam this year as last year, reversing 10-year decline, and percentage of applicants from minority groups has reached all-time high (S)
